content.content:
# Analysis of CNC Zero Hour Replays Page

## Raw HTML from First Replay `<li>` Tag

Starting from the first `<li>` in the replay list:

```html
<li>27
    
    [2708](https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?s=0&game=2&show=download&&id=290666 "Download EPIC_2v2_lol_rage_crazy_vs_size_google.rep, 284.87k") [58]
```

The opening structure is: `<li>` containing numeric content, followed by link elements with `[count]` notation wrapped in brackets.

## HTML Surrounding "Replays Found"

The 300-character context around this phrase:

```html
//<!\[CDATA\[ // INIT var base\_url\_replaysystem = "https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?s=0&game=2&"; //\]\]>

Hot Upcoming Replays

No hot upcoming replays to display.

[](https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?game=2&tab=popular&sort_by=date_added&active_tab=popular&rss "Follow these replays using your favorite feed reader!")1636 Replays Found
```

## Regarding 403 Errors on Search URLs

The `show=search_submit` parameter combined with query parameters like `army_1=all` may trigger 403 responses due to:

- **CSRF protection**: The form likely requires a session token not present in direct URL attempts
- **Referrer validation**: The site may check request origin
- **Rate limiting**: Automated search patterns can trigger security blocks
- **Form submission requirements**: Search functionality expects POST data, not GET parameters

The replay system appears designed for authenticated form submissions rather than direct parameter manipulation.

## 1. Number "27" HTML Structure
The "27" appears as bare text within an `<li>` element, not wrapped in a span with a class. The structure is: `<li>27` followed by download and post count information.

## 2. Table vs. List Elements
The page uses **exclusively `<li>` elements** for replay listings. There is no `<table>` element containing replay data anywhere on the page.

## 3. Search/Filter Form
No explicit form element with action and method attributes is visible in the provided HTML. The page appears to use URL parameters for filtering rather than a traditional form submission. Navigation occurs through query string parameters like `?s=0&game=2&tab=popular&show=index&sort_by=date_added`.

## 4. Total Replays Count
The page indicates **"1636 Replays Found"** near the pagination section, representing the total count for the current filter view.

## 5. All Replays Link
Yes. The **"Member Replays"** link (showing 12,366 replays) provides access to a broader set: `https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?s=0&game=2&tab=popular&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&display_mode=standard`

This differs from the currently displayed "Popular Replays" view (1636 total).

content.content:
# CNC Zero Hour Replays Page Analysis

## Page Purpose
This is the **CNC Zero Hour Replays** section of GameReplays.org, displaying competitive game recordings for the real-time strategy game Command & Conquer: Zero Hour.

## Total Replays Found
**1,636 replays** are available, organized across 55 pages with 30 replays per page.

## First Replay Download Link
The first replay's download href is:
```
https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?s=0&game=2&show=download&&id=368853
```

## Tab Navigation & Sections
The page features these main navigation tabs with hrefs:

- **Popular Replays**: `replays.php?game=2&tab=popular&show=index&tab_new=popular`
- **Member Replays**: `replays.php?game=2&tab=popular&show=index&tab_new=upcoming`
- **Replays of the Week (ROTW)**: `replays.php?game=2&show=rotw_replays`
- **Expert Replays**: `replays.php?game=2&show=expert_replays`
- **Gold Replays**: `replays.php?game=2&show=gold_replays`
- **Silver Replays**: `replays.php?game=2&show=silver_replays`
- **Event Replays**: `replays.php?game=2&show=events`
- **Search**: `replays.php?game=2&show=search`
- **Upload**: `replays.php?game=2&show=upload`

content.text:
Here is a complete, authoritative breakdown of everything the fetches revealed: --- ## 1. Cloudflare / Access Status The site is **not behind a Cloudflare challenge**. Pages load directly without any "Just a Moment" or `cf-browser-verification` block. However, **the original search URL you provided returns HTTP 403**. The cause is the `show=search_submit` parameter — the site appears to require either a POST submission, a valid session cookie, or a referer header to reach that endpoint. Direct GET requests to `show=search_submit` are blocked. The workaround is to use `show=index&tab_new=upcoming` instead (see #5). --- ## 2. Total Replay Count The search URL you provided 403s, so we cannot confirm its "35998" figure directly. The actual counts by tab: | Tab | URL param | Count | |---|---|---| | Popular replays | `tab_new=popular` | **1,636** replays (55 pages) | | Member replays (all) | `tab_new=upcoming` | **12,366** replays | The "35998" in your original URL may be stale or from a different filter state that requires a live session. The member-replays tab at 12,366 is the largest accessible set without authentication. --- ## 3. Exact HTML Structure of Each Replay Row Each replay is a `
  • ` element with this structure (actual HTML recovered): ```html
  • 0 3 1 saptac HARD^^ Usa mirror vs HARD By: saptac - Added 26 Mar, 2026 - v1.04
    • ``` ### CSS selectors / parsing notes There are **no classes or IDs** on the `
  • ` or its children. All fields are bare text nodes or unclassed `` and `` tags. You must parse by **position/order** within the `
  • `: | Field | How to extract | |---|---| | **Replay ID** | From any `` containing `id=XXXXXX` — e.g. `show=download&&id=369445` or `show=details&id=369445` | | **Title** | The bare text node near the bottom of `
  • `, before the "By:" text | | **Uploader** | `` whose href contains `show=member_replays&member_id=` | | **Date** | Text matching `Added DD Mon, YYYY` in the "By: ... - Added ... - v..." string | | **Version** | Text matching `v1.04` etc. in the same "By:" string | | **Faction 1** | First `` — `alt` attribute gives faction name (e.g. `"Vanilla USA"`, `"China Nuke"`, `"USA SW"`) | | **Faction 2** | Second `` — same pattern | | **Player 1** | `` immediately after first ``, href contains `search_player=` | | **Player 2** | `` immediately after second ``, href contains `search_player=` | | **Download count** | First `` in the `
  • `, href contains `show=download` — inner text is the count | | **Comment count** | Second `` in the `
  • `, href contains `show=details` — inner text is the count | | **Wubs** | **First text node** in the `
  • ` before any `` — a bare integer (e.g. `0`, `5`, `27`) with no wrapping tag | Army images live at `/community/replaysystem/games_files/2/armies/.png`. Known filenames: `usa.png`, `laser.png`, `nuke.png`, `m_superweapon.png`, `china.png`, `random.png`, etc. --- ## 4. Pagination - **Page size:** 30 replays per page - **Offset parameter:** `st=` (integer, multiples of 30) - **Page 1:** `st=0`, **Page 2:** `st=30`, **Page 3:** `st=60`, etc. - **Total pages for member replays:** 12366 / 30 = ~413 pages Example page-2 URL: ``` https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?s=0&game=2&tab=upcoming&show=index&sort_by=date_added&active_tab=upcoming&st=30 ``` --- ## 5. Working URL Structure (search URL needs correction) The original search URL with `show=search_submit` returns 403. **Use this pattern instead:** ``` https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php?game=2&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st=0 ``` Available `sort_by` values (confirmed working): - `date_added` — chronological newest first - `bump_date` — last activity - `wubs` — sorted by wub count - `downloads` — by download count For army/faction filtering: the `army_1=` and `army_2=` parameters only work when submitted through the search form with a live session. For a scraper, you'd need to either establish a session first, or filter by faction client-side after fetching the full listing. --- ## Summary for your scraper - Fetch `show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st={offset}` in increments of 30 - Parse `
  • ` elements within the replay list; all fields are positional (no classes) - Replay ID: extract from `id=` in any `` per row - Wubs: first text node in `
  • ` (bare integer, no wrapper) - Faction: `` text - The site does not block direct GET requests on the index/listing URLs — no Cloudflare, no session required for browsing
